message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Anime Tactical Simulator Eternal Units: Tier List & Guide 2026",
  description: "Master the Anime Tactical Simulator eternal units meta. Explore our 2026 tier list, evolution strategies, and world progression tips for Roblox.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/fff?text=Anime+Tactical+Simulator+Units",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Anime Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Anime tactical simulator eternal units**: Focus on obtaining Secret and Tactical rarity units like Jin Woo and Igris for endgame dominance.
- **Tower Mode Priority**: Reach Floor 100 as fast as possible to unlock maximum unit deployment slots.
- **Resource Management**: Save your Gems for multi-summons on high-value banners and avoid early-game trait gambling.
- **Evolution Mechanic**: Use the Soul Transfer system to recycle lower-tier units into essential evolution materials.
- **Raid Strategy**: Farm Stat Cubes in raids only after securing a full team of level 50+ Mythic units.
</Callout>

The landscape of Roblox anime simulators has shifted dramatically in 2026, with players seeking more depth than the standard clicker formula. Understanding the **anime tactical simulator eternal units** meta is the difference between being stuck in starter worlds and dominating the Solo Leveling endgame. Unlike traditional simulators that rely on raw power, this title introduces tactical positioning and card-based summoning that requires a strategic mindset. Whether you are transitioning from older titles or starting fresh, mastering unit synergies and the evolution path is essential. In this comprehensive guide, we will break down the highest-tier units, the most efficient world progression routes, and how to maximize your squad's damage output through raids and tower climbs. By following our expert strategies, you will transform your roster into an unstoppable force capable of clearing the most difficult dungeons in the game.

<Section icon={Map} title="Understanding Unit Rarity and Summoning Mechanics">

The core of your power comes from the units you deploy. In Anime Tactical Simulator, the summoning system uses "Cards" rather than the traditional "Stars" found in games like Anime Eternal. This visual shift represents a more polished tactical approach to squad building.

**Video Highlights:**
- Comparison between tactical gameplay and traditional anime simulators.
- Visual breakdown of the card-based summoning system and pity mechanics.
- Preview of high-tier units including Gojo and Jin Woo in action.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="ExK6gTYpb7s" title="Anime Tactical Simulator Gameplay Preview" />

Units are categorized by rarity, and while Legendaries will carry you through World 1 (Planet Namek), you must pivot to Mythics and Secrets as soon as possible. The "Tactical" rarity represents the absolute pinnacle of unit power, often featuring unique AoE circles and ability meters.

| Rarity | Pull Chance | Primary Use | Strategy |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Tactical** | 0.002% | Endgame Bossing | Keep at all costs / Max Evolve |
| **Secret** | <0.01% | High-tier Raids | Main DPS for World 3+ |
| **Mythic** | 0.2% - 0.5% | Core Team Base | Use for early evolution fodder |
| **Legendary** | 5% - 10% | Early Progression | Sell for Souls or use as XP |
| **Rare/Common** | High | Fodder | Auto-fuse for unit levels |

<Callout type="tip" title="Summoning Tip">
Always utilize the "Multi-Open" feature once unlocked through achievements. It significantly speeds up the process of hitting your 1.5k Mythic pity, ensuring you don't waste time on single pulls.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="The Eternal Units Tier List for 2026">

The current meta for **anime tactical simulator eternal units** is dominated by characters with high AoE damage and scaling abilities. Units from the Solo Leveling and Demon Slayer worlds currently sit at the top of the power curve due to their superior base stats and ability multipliers.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="S-Tier: The Dominators">
    - **Jin Woo (Secret)**: Unparalleled scaling and shadow summons.
    - **Igris (Tactical)**: Massive AoE damage and high crit rates.
    - **Gojo (Exclusive)**: Crowd control and infinite void mechanics.
  </Card>
  <Card title="A-Tier: Reliable DPS">
    - **Kokushibo**: Exceptional bleed damage for long boss fights.
    - **Yurichi**: High speed and multi-hit capabilities.
    - **Free Ren**: Strong utility and ranged magic attacks.
  </Card>
  <Card title="B-Tier: Niche/Support">
    - **Aqua**: Essential for healing in high-level dungeons.
    - **Vegeta**: Solid mid-game damage for Namek and Coliseum.
    - **Luffy**: Decent knockback but falls off in endgame raids.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

When building your squad, focus on the "Energy" output. High energy units allow for more frequent ability usage, which is critical in timed challenges like the "Double Dungeon."

| Unit Name | Rarity | Best World | Key Strength |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Jin Woo** | Secret | Solo Leveling | Infinite scaling / Shadow Army |
| **Igris** | Tactical | Solo Leveling | Critical strike / Massive AoE |
| **Kokushibo** | Mythic | Demon Slayer | Bleed stacks / High durability |
| **Yurichi** | Mythic | Demon Slayer | Speed blitz / Multi-target |
| **Gojo** | Tactical | Exclusive | Stun mechanics / High base DMG |

<Callout type="warning" title="Meta Warning">
Do not over-invest in World 1 or 2 Mythics. The power creep in World 4 (Solo Leveling) is significant; save your Stat Cubes for units obtained in the later stages of the game.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="World Progression and Unlock Strategy">

Efficiency is key to reaching the endgame. Many players make the mistake of grinding bosses in early worlds for too long. Instead, focus on completing the main quest chains to unlock the next map, which offers significantly higher Yen and XP rewards.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Planet Namek Rush">
    Complete all kill quests immediately. Do not stay to farm Shenron unless you have 7 Dragon Balls from natural progression. Unlock the Coliseum as soon as you have the required Yen.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Coliseum Unit Building">
    This is where you should aim for your first full Mythic team. The card packs here are affordable and offer a significant jump in power over the Namek units.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Demon Slayer Raid Prep">
    Unlock the Abandoned Village. Start participating in raids to collect Medals. These are required for the trait gambling system which becomes necessary for the final world.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="Solo Leveling Endgame">
    The final hurdle. Enemies here have massive HP pools. You will need at least one Tactical or Secret unit to clear the "Double Dungeon" and unlock the Awakening system.
  </Step>
</Steps>

| World Name | Unlock Cost | Key Feature | Priority Drop |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Planet Namek** | Free | Shenron Wishes | Dragon Balls |
| **Coliseum** | 1M Yen | Multi-Summon | Mythic Luffy/Zoro |
| **Demon Slayer** | 50M Yen | Trait Gambling | Shadow Medals |
| **Solo Leveling** | 500M Yen | Awakening Dungeon | Stat Cubes / Igris |

<Callout type="success" title="Pro Progression Tip">
Use the "Travel" menu to quickly move between worlds. Unlike other simulators, this feature is not locked behind a paywall, allowing you to farm specific world bosses efficiently.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="Tower Mode and Permanent Upgrades">

Tower Mode is the most important side-content for any serious player. It provides the only way to increase your unit deployment slots without spending Robux. Each extra slot represents a massive multiplier to your total team damage.

<Checklist
  id="tower-milestones"
  title="Critical Tower Floor Goals:"
  items={[
    "Reach Floor 10 for +1 Unit Slot",
    "Reach Floor 25 for +1 Unit Slot",
    "Reach Floor 50 for Rare Item Drops",
    "Reach Floor 100 for +1 Unit Slot and Secret Unit"
  ]}
/>

Beyond slots, the Tower is your primary source of Gems. Gems should be reinvested into the "Talent" system. Talents provide account-wide buffs that apply to all **anime tactical simulator eternal units**, regardless of which world they originated from.

| Talent Type | Effect | Priority | Source |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Attack DMG** | Increases base power | High | Stat Cubes |
| **Crit Chance** | Better for endgame | Medium | Stat Cubes |
| **Cooldown** | Faster ability use | Very High | Stat Cubes |
| **Luck Boost** | Better summon odds | Low | Gems |

<Callout type="info" title="Stat Cube Usage">
Stat Cubes are rare. Only use them on "Perfect Talent" rolls for your Secret or Tactical units. Using them on Legendaries is a waste of endgame potential.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Evolution and the Soul Transfer System">

Evolution is the process of taking a base unit and exponentially increasing its stats. In Anime Tactical Simulator, this requires three components: Duplicate Units, Souls, and Evolution Stones.

1. **Duplicate Units**: You need multiple copies of the same unit to reach higher star levels.
2. **Souls**: Obtained by selling units of the same rarity. For example, selling a Mythic unit gives you Mythic Souls.
3. **Evolution Stones**: Rare drops from high-level raids and the Awakening Dungeon.

**Evolution Star Scaling:**
- **1-Star**: Base damage +0%
- **2-Star**: Base damage +25%
- **3-Star**: Base damage +75% (100% Total)
- **4-Star+**: Unlocks unique ability modifiers and VFX.

| Material | How to Obtain | Best Use |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Mythic Souls** | Selling Mythic Units | Evolving S-Tier DPS |
| **Legendary Souls** | Selling Legendaries | Evolving Support Units |
| **Stat Cubes** | Raids / Dungeons | Upgrading Talents |
| **Dungeon Keys** | Daily / Codes | Entering Awakening Dungeons |

<Callout type="warning" title="Evolution Warning">
Never sell a Shiny unit for Souls. Shiny units have inherently higher base stats and should always be the base for your evolution, not the fodder.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "How do I get the best anime tactical simulator eternal units fast?",
    answer: "The fastest way is to redeem all active codes (like RELEASE and UPDATE1) to get Gems and Dungeon Keys. Use these to summon on the Solo Leveling banner and push Tower Mode for extra unit slots."
  },
  {
    question: "Is it better to fuse or sell units?",
    answer: "Fuse Common and Rare units to level up your main squad. Sell Epic and Legendary units to acquire the Souls needed for high-tier evolution."
  },
  {
    question: "When should I start farming Raids?",
    answer: "Wait until you have a full team of at least 4 Mythic units at Level 50. Rushing raids too early is inefficient as the bosses have high HP and the rewards scale with clear speed."
  },
  {
    question: "Which gamepasses are worth the Robux?",
    answer: "The +2 Units Equip and Card Access (open cards anywhere) are the highest value. Luck passes are helpful but secondary to deployment slot increases."
  }
]} />

</Section>
